summary: The implementation partially fulfills the BuildRequest. User registration with unique code generation (REQ-1), code-based connection system (REQ-2), and modern UI design with coral/teal colors (REQ-4) are implemented. However, the core chat messaging functionality (REQ-3) is incomplete—the ChatPage exists only as a placeholder acknowledging missing backend methods for sending/receiving messages. REQ-5 is partially implemented (code display after registration) but not prominently shown in the main interface.

missing_requirements.requirement: Build a one-to-one chat interface where connected users can send and receive text messages in real-time
missing_requirements.reason: ChatPage.tsx is only a placeholder stating 'Chat Feature Coming Soon' and 'Backend Integration Required'. No backend methods exist for sending or retrieving messages. The AC requirements for sending text messages, displaying conversation threads, and preserving chat history are not implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Display the user's own unique code prominently in the interface so they can share it with others
missing_requirements.reason: The code is only shown on the RegisterPage after registration, not prominently displayed in the main interface for ongoing access. AC specifies code should be 'visible in the main interface' for easy sharing at any time, but it's only shown once during registration flow.

hallucinated_features.description: Backend stores display names for users in addition to unique codes
hallucinated_features.reason: The User type in backend/main.mo includes a 'displayName' field and the registerUser function accepts displayName as a parameter. The BuildRequest only specifies unique numeric codes, with no mention of display names or user profile customization.

hallucinated_features.description: getAllUsers query method returning all registered users
hallucinated_features.reason: backend/main.mo implements a public query function getAllUsers() that returns an array of all users. The BuildRequest does not specify any requirement for listing or browsing all users—only connecting via known codes.
